name: Alert State
description: Alert State is a concealment or detection mechanic that lets players manage information, sound, sight, and risk.
design_notes: Alert State should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For stealth mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use alert state as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect alert state to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels observant and controlled while avoiding or manipulating threats.

parameters.name: detection_threshold
parameters.description: Amount of suspicion required before AI reacts.

parameters.name: decay_rate
parameters.description: How quickly suspicion or noise fades.

parameters.name: feedback_delay
parameters.description: Delay before the player receives detection feedback.

edge_cases: Alert State is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Alert State overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Alert State runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Alert State state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Alert State plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Alert State produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Alert State strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Alert State becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Alert State with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Alert State as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Alert State with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Alert State as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
